texto: Who ever fights monsters should see to it that in the process he does not become a monster.
explicacao: Orientação direta de James O'Barr sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “Who ever fights monsters should see to it that in the process he does not become a monster.” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, monsters, the-crow, útil em momentos de the-crow.

texto: Here dwells a snake, one thousand miles longCoiled, one thousand miles deepEyes like candy, it has eyes like candyHard and blue, but soft as kittens feetOut of sight or in the element of lightIt could be a devil, it could be an angelWith spiders inside a vision from hellIts spine is a vertical screamSlow as concrete, blurred as a dreamFueled by inertia, depth, radius, and velocity,Its soul--a twisted wreckage of despair and painAnd the spiders inside are just praying for rainKilling time killing timeAnd praying for rainOne thousand miles deep
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a James O'Barr: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Here dwells a snake, one thousand miles longCoiled, one thousand miles deepEyes like candy, it ha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, despair, good-and-evil pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e good-and-evil.

texto: People once believed that when someone dies, a crow carries their soul to the land of the dead. But sometimes, something so bad happens that a terrible sadness is carried with it and the soul can't rest. Then sometimes, just sometimes, the crow can bring that soul back to put the wrong things right.
explicacao: Citação de James O'Barr sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “People once believed that when someone dies, a crow carries their soul to the land of the dead. B…” articula reflexao, crow, death em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como luto e death.

frase_original: People once believed that when someone dies, a crow carries their soul to the land of the dead. But sometimes, something so bad happens that a terrible sadness is carried with it and the soul can't rest. Then sometimes, just sometimes, the crow can bring that soul back to put the wrong things right.
